backlog.title: Bug Fix Implementation — Config Bootstrap, Upgrade Backup, and Minor Polish
backlog.description: Fix 6 issues found during end-to-end testing: 1 Critical (bootstrap stager-fallback writes invalid bare pi model), 2 Major (commented-out pi block invalid models; config upgrade missing backup), 3 Minor (token-limit sub-270 floor violation; doubled stagecoach: prefix; auto-stage grammar). All fixes are grounded in the architecture research in plan/015_b461e4720495/bugfix/001_3c8c5ced7ac1/architecture/.

milestones.title: Critical — Bootstrap Stager-Fallback Bare Pi Model (Issue 1)
milestones.description: Fix the config bootstrap's stager-fallback path so that when a non-stager-capable provider (agy, opencode, qwen-code, codex, cursor) is the target, and the stager role falls back to pi (a provider_flag / multi-backend provider), the stager model is written BLANK (not bare 'gpt-5.4-mini'). Add a post-bootstrap ValidateModel regression net that catches this class of bug automatically. Root cause: piBlanked only fires when target=='pi', not when pi is the stager fallback. See architecture/bootstrap_pi_model_bug.md.

tasks.title: Fix stager-fallback to blank pi model in buildBootstrapConfig (Issue 1)
tasks.description: In internal/config/bootstrap.go buildBootstrapConfig(), after the StagerFallback() call returns (stagerName, stagerModel), add a guard: if stagerName == 'pi' AND stagerName != target (i.e. pi was chosen as the stager fallback for a non-pi target), blank stagerModel to ''. Emit the same multi-backend guidance comment on the stager block as the target==pi path uses. Update the test assertion at bootstrap_test.go:87 from 'gpt-5.4-mini' to blank. Extend the negative gpt-5.4 guard to the stager-fallback test cases.

subtasks.title: Add stager-fallback blanking guard and guidance comment in buildBootstrapConfig
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/bootstrap_pi_model_bug.md §Issue 1. The bug is at internal/config/bootstrap.go:174-181. `piBlanked := target == "pi"` (line 165) is ONLY true when target IS pi. When target is agy/opencode/qwen-code/codex/cursor, `StagerFallback()` (bootstrap.go:76-89) returns `("pi", "gpt-5.4-mini")` — a FRESH bare model from DefaultModelsForProvider("pi") at role_defaults.go:96. The `if piBlanked { stagerModel = "" }` guard at line 175 does NOT fire because target != "pi". The result is `[role.stager]` with `provider = "pi"` + bare `model = "gpt-5.4-mini"` — a hard FR-R5b error.
2. INPUT: The existing `buildBootstrapConfig(target string, installed []string, overrides map[string]string) string` function at bootstrap.go:143. The `stagerName, stagerModel := StagerFallback(target, models)` call at line 174 returns `("pi", "gpt-5.4-mini")` for non-pi non-stager targets. The `writeRoleBlock(b, role, prov, model, annotation)` helper at bootstrap.go:108-116 writes the [role.*] block with an optional annotation comment.
3. LOGIC: After the `StagerFallback()` call (line 174) and after the existing `if piBlanked { stagerModel = "" }` guard (line 175), add a new guard: `if stagerName == "pi" && stagerName != target { stagerModel = "" }`. Then, for the stager block's annotation (currently computed at lines 192-194 as `stagerAnnotation`), when the stager fell back to pi AND the model was blanked, append the multi-backend guidance text (same wording as the target==pi guidance at lines 186-189: 'pi is a multi-backend provider — prefix the model with your inference backend, e.g. model = "zai/glm-5.2". A bare model (no "/") on pi is a config error (FR-R5b).'). This guidance should appear as the annotation comment on the [role.stager] block via writeRoleBlock's annotation parameter. Do NOT change the stagerName routing (pi remains the stager provider) — only the MODEL is blanked.
4. OUTPUT: buildBootstrapConfig() now produces `model = ""` for [role.stager] when stager falls back to pi, with a guidance annotation comment. The function still returns valid TOML. For consumption by P1.M1.T1.S3 (test updates) and P1.M1.T2.S1 (ValidateModel regression test).
5. DOCS: none — the fix makes the implementation match the EXISTING documented behavior (docs/configuration.md:40 already says pi models are left EMPTY). No user-facing/config/API surface change beyond correcting the output to match docs.

subtasks.title: Update test assertions and extend negative gpt-5.4 guard for stager-fallback cases
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/bootstrap_pi_model_bug.md §Issue 1, §Test That Pins the Bug. The test at internal/config/bootstrap_test.go:74-97 (`TestBuildBootstrapConfig_AgyStagerFallback`) currently PINS the buggy value at line 87: `assertContains(t, content, "[role.stager]", \`model = "gpt-5.4-mini"\`)`. The negative guard at lines 41-43 (`if strings.Contains(content, "gpt-5.4") { t.Errorf(...) }`) only runs for `target=="pi"` — NOT for the stager-fallback cases.
2. INPUT: The fixed buildBootstrapConfig() from P1.M1.T1.S1, which now writes `model = ""` for [role.stager] when stager falls back to pi.
3. LOGIC: In `TestBuildBootstrapConfig_AgyStagerFallback` (bootstrap_test.go:74-97): (a) Change line 87 from `assertContains(t, content, "[role.stager]", \`model = "gpt-5.4-mini"\`)` to `assertContains(t, content, "[role.stager]", \`model = ""\`)`. (b) Add a negative guard after the stager assertions: `if strings.Contains(content, "gpt-5.4") { t.Errorf("agy bootstrap must not ship un-routable bare gpt-5.4* pi models; got:\n%s", content) }` — this mirrors the existing pi-target negative guard at lines 41-43. (c) Assert the multi-backend guidance comment is present on the stager block. Also add a test case for `buildBootstrapConfig("opencode", nil, nil)` with the same assertions (stager routed to pi, model blanked, no bare gpt-5.4). Run `go test ./internal/config/ -v -run TestBuildBootstrapConfig_AgyStagerFallback` to verify.
4. OUTPUT: Updated bootstrap_test.go that passes with the fixed buildBootstrapConfig and would fail with the old buggy version. For consumption by P1.M1.T2.S1 (ValidateModel regression net).
5. DOCS: none — test-only changes, no user-facing surface.

tasks.title: Post-bootstrap ValidateModel regression net (catches Issue 1 & 2 class of bugs automatically)
tasks.description: Add a test that generates bootstrap configs for every (target, installed) combination, parses the active (uncommented) [role.*] blocks, resolves each role's provider+model, and calls Manifest.ValidateModel on the model. Any non-nil result = test failure. This would have caught Issue 1 immediately (bare 'gpt-5.4-mini' on pi fails ValidateModel) and provides a permanent regression net against future bootstrap changes that introduce invalid models.

subtasks.title: Add post-bootstrap ValidateModel test for all (target, installed) combinations
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/bootstrap_pi_model_bug.md §Post-Bootstrap ValidateModel Regression Net, and architecture/test_patterns.md §Provider Tests. `Manifest.ValidateModel(model string) error` lives at internal/provider/manifest.go:136 and enforces FR-R5b (bare model on a provider_flag provider is a hard error). The test must generate a bootstrap config, parse the ACTIVE [role.*] blocks (not the commented ones), resolve each role's effective (provider, model) using config.ResolveRoleModel (internal/config/roles.go:42), then call ValidateModel. IMPORTANT: `internal/config` MUST NOT import `internal/provider` (the config/provider decoupling invariant). The test must use an EXTERNAL test package (`package config_test`) so it can import both `config` and `provider`. Or construct a minimal provider.Manifest directly. Existing pattern: TestValidateModel_BareModelOnProviderFlagProvider_Errors at manifest_test.go:318 constructs a Manifest with ProviderFlag set and calls ValidateModel. The built-in manifests are available via provider.BuiltinManifests (exported).
2. INPUT: The fixed buildBootstrapConfig from P1.M1.T1.S1 (produces blank pi stager models). The existing TestBuildBootstrapConfig_ValidTOML test (bootstrap_test.go:144-162) already iterates over (target, installed) combinations — use the same table. config.ResolveRoleModel(role, cfg) from roles.go:42 returns (provider, model, reasoning). provider.BuiltinManifests provides the manifest for each provider name.
3. LOGIC: Create a new test file (e.g. `internal/config/bootstrap_validate_test.go` in `package config_test`). For each (target, installed) combo from the ValidTOML table: (a) Call config.GenerateBootstrapConfig(target) to get the TOML string. (b) Parse it with toml.Unmarshal into a config.Config (the Load path does this; or use a minimal parse). (c) For each role in ["planner", "stager", "message", "arbiter"]: resolve (provider, model) via config.ResolveRoleModel(role, cfg). (d) If model == "" skip (blank is valid — the user fills it in). (e) If model != "", look up the provider's manifest via provider.BuiltinManifests[provider], call manifest.ValidateModel(model). (f) Assert no error. Also test with installed = nil (the no-detection case) and installed = [all providers]. The COMMENTED blocks should NOT be validated (they are inert TOML comments, not parsed). Run `go test ./internal/config/ -v -run TestBootstrapValidateModels`.
4. OUTPUT: A new test file that validates every active role model in every bootstrap output. Catches Issue 1 (bare pi stager), Issue 2 (bare pi in commented blocks — though these are comments, the test validates only ACTIVE roles). Provides a permanent regression net.
5. DOCS: none — test-only, no user-facing surface.

milestones.title: Major — Commented Pi Block & Config Upgrade Backup (Issues 2 & 3)
milestones.description: Fix two Major issues: (Issue 2) the commented-out pi provider block in every config init output uses invalid bare models — uncommenting produces an FR-R5b hard error; (Issue 3) config upgrade does not create a backup of the prior config file, violating FR-B8. See architecture/bootstrap_pi_model_bug.md §Issue 2 and architecture/config_upgrade_backup.md.

tasks.title: Fix commented-out pi provider block to use blank/prefixed models (Issue 2)
tasks.description: In the commented-block generation loop of buildBootstrapConfig (bootstrap.go:205-222), when the provider is pi (a provider_flag provider), write the models as blank with a guidance comment, OR write placeholder-prefixed example models. Any uncommented value must be FR-R5b-valid.

subtasks.title: Modify commented-block loop to blank pi models with guidance comment
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/bootstrap_pi_model_bug.md §Issue 2. The commented-block loop is at internal/config/bootstrap.go:205-222. It calls `writeCommentedRoleBlock(b, role, prov, model)` (bootstrap.go:119-123) which writes `# [role.X]`, `# provider = "pi"`, `# model = "gpt-5.4"`. For pi, `other := DefaultModelsForProvider(name)` (line 210) returns bare models. These are COMMENTED so they don't error at load time — but FR-B1 says uncommenting should produce a working config. The opencode commented block correctly uses `openai/gpt-5.4` (prefixed); the bug is specific to pi.
2. INPUT: buildBootstrapConfig() from P1.M1.T1.S1 (already fixes the stager-fallback path; this subtask fixes the SEPARATE commented-block loop). The `writeCommentedRoleBlock` helper at bootstrap.go:119-123. The `DefaultModelsForProvider` function at role_defaults.go:96.
3. LOGIC: In the commented-block loop (bootstrap.go:205-222), when `name == "pi"`: (a) Before the four `writeCommentedRoleBlock` calls, emit a guidance comment line after the section header: `# NOTE: pi is a multi-backend provider — prefix the model with your inference backend,` / `# e.g. model = "zai/gpt-5.4". A bare model (no "/") on pi is a config error (FR-R5b).` (b) Write the models as blank: `# model = ""` — mirroring the target==pi path's blanking. This means modifying the loop to detect pi and either pass `""` as the model to `writeCommentedRoleBlock`, or use a variant that adds the guidance. The simplest approach: add `if name == "pi" { for k := range other { other[k] = "" } }` right after `other := DefaultModelsForProvider(name)` at line 210, and emit the guidance comment before the writeCommentedRoleBlock calls. This mirrors how piBlanked blanks the target's models.
4. OUTPUT: The commented pi block now reads `# model = ""` for all four roles with a guidance comment. Uncommenting produces a valid (though model-less) config that the user fills in. Contrast with the buggy output that wrote bare `gpt-5.4*` values. For consumption by P1.M2.T1.S2 (test).
5. DOCS: Mode A — update docs/providers.md:125 (the note about pi models being blank in bootstrap) to clarify that BOTH the active block AND the commented-out pi block use blank models. The existing text says 'EXCEPT for pi, whose per-role models are written EMPTY' — this should be verified/extended to note the commented blocks are also blanked.

subtasks.title: Add test for commented-out pi block FR-R5b validity
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/test_patterns.md §Bootstrap Tests. The existing `TestBuildBootstrapConfig_OtherInstalledCommented` at bootstrap_test.go:101-127 tests the commented claude block. It asserts `# model = "haiku"` (claude's bare model — valid because claude has no ProviderFlag). We need a similar test for the commented pi block.
2. INPUT: The fixed buildBootstrapConfig from P1.M2.T1.S1, which now writes `# model = ""` for the commented pi block with a guidance comment.
3. LOGIC: Add a test `TestBuildBootstrapConfig_CommentedPiBlockBlanked` that calls `buildBootstrapConfig("claude", []string{"claude", "pi"}, nil)` (target=claude, pi installed). Assert: (a) The commented pi block header `# === pi (installed)` is present. (b) The pi block contains `# model = ""` (NOT `# model = "gpt-5.4"`). (c) The pi block contains the multi-backend guidance comment. (d) Negative: the pi block must NOT contain `gpt-5.4` anywhere. Also run the existing `TestBuildBootstrapConfig_OtherInstalledCommented` to ensure the claude block still works (claude models are NOT blanked — claude has no ProviderFlag). Run `go test ./internal/config/ -v -run TestBuildBootstrapConfig_CommentedPi`.
4. OUTPUT: Updated bootstrap_test.go with the commented-pi-block test. Passes with the fix, fails with the old code.
5. DOCS: none — test-only.

tasks.title: Add timestamped backup to config upgrade write path (Issue 3, FR-B8)
tasks.description: In internal/cmd/config.go runConfigUpgrade(), insert a config.WriteTimestampedBackup(path) call before the os.WriteFile that overwrites the config, mirroring the writeBootstrapFile pattern used by config init --force. Add test assertions that a .bak.* file exists after upgrade.

subtasks.title: Insert WriteTimestampedBackup before os.WriteFile in runConfigUpgrade
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/config_upgrade_backup.md. The bug is at internal/cmd/config.go:182-189 in `runConfigUpgrade`. After the `if !changed { return nil }` check (line 182), the function writes directly via `os.WriteFile(path, []byte(newContent), 0o644)` (line 186) with NO preceding backup. The correct pattern is at writeBootstrapFile (config.go:504-533), which calls `config.WriteTimestampedBackup(path)` before the write (lines 521-528). WriteTimestampedBackup (backup.go:18) returns ("", nil) if file doesn't exist, (backupPath, nil) on success, ("", error) on failure. No new import needed — `internal/config` is already imported; `fmt` and `exitcode` are in scope.
2. INPUT: `runConfigUpgrade(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error` at internal/cmd/config.go:157. The `config.WriteTimestampedBackup(path string) (string, error)` function from internal/config/backup.go. The `cmd.OutOrStderr()` writer for the backup notice message.
3. LOGIC: In `runConfigUpgrade`, between the `if !changed { … return nil }` block (line 182) and the `os.WriteFile` call (line 186), insert: `if backup, berr := config.WriteTimestampedBackup(path); berr != nil { return exitcode.New(exitcode.Error, fmt.Errorf("backup existing config %s: %w", path, berr)) } else if backup != "" { fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStderr(), "Backed up previous config to %s\n", backup) }`. This mirrors writeBootstrapFile lines 521-528 exactly. The backup runs ONLY when `changed == true` (a real write happens). The inert (line 174), already-current (line 182), malformed-TOML, and no-file paths all return early without reaching the backup code.
4. OUTPUT: runConfigUpgrade now creates a `<file>.bak.<timestamp>` backup before overwriting, identical to config init --force. The upgrade output includes a 'Backed up previous config to …' notice on stderr. For consumption by P1.M2.T2.S2 (test assertions).
5. DOCS: none — the backup is transparent user-facing behavior, not a config/API surface change. The FR-B8 requirement is already documented in the PRD.

subtasks.title: Add backup file assertions to TestConfigUpgrade_OlderUpdated and V2ToV3Rewrite
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/config_upgrade_backup.md §Existing Tests. The backup assertion pattern exists ONLY for config init --force at internal/cmd/config_test.go:631-634, 675-678: `matches, _ := filepath.Glob(filepath.Join(globalDir, "config.toml.bak.*")); if len(matches) == 0 { t.Errorf(...) }`. The upgrade tests (TestConfigUpgrade_OlderUpdated at line 1137, TestConfigUpgrade_V2ToV3Rewrite at line 1423) have NO backup assertions. The TestConfigUpgrade_AlreadyCurrent (line 1110) and TestConfigUpgrade_InertFile_NoOp (line 1235) should NOT create backups (they return early).
2. INPUT: The fixed runConfigUpgrade from P1.M2.T2.S1, which now creates a .bak.* file before overwriting.
3. LOGIC: In `TestConfigUpgrade_OlderUpdated` (config_test.go:1137) and `TestConfigUpgrade_V2ToV3Rewrite` (config_test.go:1423), after the Execute + upgrade assertion, add: `matches, _ := filepath.Glob(filepath.Join(globalDir, "config.toml.bak.*")); if len(matches) == 0 { t.Errorf("no timestamped backup created after config upgrade (FR-B8)") }`. Optionally also assert the backup file content equals the pre-upgrade content. Verify TestConfigUpgrade_AlreadyCurrent and TestConfigUpgrade_InertFile_NoOp still pass (they should NOT create backups since they return early). Run `go test ./internal/cmd/ -v -run TestConfigUpgrade`.
4. OUTPUT: Updated config_test.go with backup assertions. Passes with the fix, fails without it.
5. DOCS: none — test-only.

milestones.title: Minor Fixes — Token Gate Floor, Doubled Prefix, Auto-Stage Grammar (Issues 4, 5, 6)
milestones.description: Fix three Minor issues: (Issue 4) FR3j token-limit invariant violated for very small token_limit values — add irreducible floor rejection; (Issue 5) doubled 'stagecoach:' prefix in --edit empty-message abort; (Issue 6) auto-stage notice uses '(1 files)' grammar. All three are small, localized fixes. See architecture/minor_fixes.md.

tasks.title: Token gate irreducible floor rejection (Issue 4, FR3j)
tasks.description: Add a floor check at each opts.TokenLimit > 0 call site in internal/git/git.go that rejects token_limit values below the computed irreducible floor (EstimateTokens(skeleton) + promptReserve + tokenBudgetMargin) with a clear error, instead of silently violating the FR3j 'always' invariant.

subtasks.title: Export IrreducibleFloor helper and add floor check at git.go call sites
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/minor_fixes.md §Issue 4. The closed-loop gate at internal/git/tokengate.go:195-216 returns the best attempt after maxClosedLoopPasses=4 even if the assembled prompt still exceeds token_limit. The degenerate case fires when bodyBudget <= 0 (tokengate.go:138): bodyBudget = tokenLimit - skeletonTokens - promptReserve - tokenBudgetMargin. Constants: tokenBudgetMargin=1024 (tokengate.go:48), minBodyTokens=8 (tokengate.go:75). Three call sites in internal/git/git.go call closedLoopGate inside `if opts.TokenLimit > 0` branches: StagedDiff at line 1058, TreeDiff at line 1583, WorkingTreeDiff at line 1758. At each call site, `skeleton` and `opts.PromptReserveTokens` are available. `EstimateTokens` is an in-package function in internal/git. The three functions return (string, error).
2. INPUT: `internal/git/tokengate.go` with constants `tokenBudgetMargin`, `EstimateTokens`, `applyWaterFillGate`, `closedLoopGate`. `internal/git/git.go` with three `closedLoopGate` call sites. The `StagedDiffOptions` struct with `TokenLimit int`, `PromptReserveTokens int`, `MeasureAssembled func(string) int` fields.
3. LOGIC: (a) In tokengate.go, export a helper: `func IrreducibleFloor(skeleton string, promptReserve int) int { return EstimateTokens(skeleton) + promptReserve + tokenBudgetMargin }`. Export `TokenBudgetMargin` as a constant alias if needed (currently unexported `tokenBudgetMargin`). (b) In git.go, at EACH of the three `if opts.TokenLimit > 0` branches, before the `closedLoopGate(...)` call, add: `floor := IrreducibleFloor(skeleton, opts.PromptReserveTokens); if opts.TokenLimit < floor { return "", fmt.Errorf("token_limit %d is below the irreducible prompt floor %d (system prompt + numstat skeleton + framing); raise it to at least %d", opts.TokenLimit, floor, floor) }`. The error propagates through the (string, error) return. (c) This does NOT change closedLoopGate's signature — the check is purely at the caller. (d) Make sure the error message clearly states the floor value so the user knows what to set.
4. OUTPUT: git.go now rejects sub-floor token_limit values with a clear error at all three diff functions. The FR3j 'always' invariant is preserved by failing loud instead of silently violating it. For consumption by P1.M3.T1.S2 (test).
5. DOCS: Mode A — update docs/configuration.md:167 (the token_limit description) to document the floor rejection: add a note that token_limit values below the irreducible floor (system prompt + skeleton + framing, which varies per run) are rejected with an error. The current text says 'a closed-loop guarantee (§9.1 FR3j) that the payload never exceeds token_limit' — append 'For extremely small limits (below the irreducible prompt floor), stagecoach rejects the limit with an error rather than silently violating the guarantee.'

subtasks.title: Add test for sub-floor token_limit rejection
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/minor_fixes.md §Issue 4 and architecture/test_patterns.md. Existing token gate tests are in internal/git/tokengate_test.go. The test needs to verify that when token_limit is below the irreducible floor, the diff functions return an error rather than an over-limit prompt.
2. INPUT: The IrreducibleFloor helper and floor check from P1.M3.T1.S1. The git.go StagedDiff/TreeDiff/WorkingTreeDiff functions that now reject sub-floor limits.
3. LOGIC: In tokengate_test.go (or a new git_test.go test), add a test that: (a) Calls IrreducibleFloor with a known skeleton and promptReserve, computes the floor. (b) Sets opts.TokenLimit to floor - 1 (below the floor). (c) Calls one of the diff functions (StagedDiff or the most testable one) and asserts it returns an error containing 'below the irreducible prompt floor'. (d) Also test that opts.TokenLimit = floor (exactly at the floor) does NOT error — it should succeed (the floor is the minimum valid value). (e) Test that opts.TokenLimit = floor + 100 succeeds normally. Run `go test ./internal/git/ -v -run TestTokenLimitFloor`.
4. OUTPUT: Updated tokengate_test.go (or git_test.go) with floor rejection tests. Passes with the fix.
5. DOCS: none — test-only.

tasks.title: Fix doubled 'stagecoach:' prefix in --edit empty-message abort (Issue 5)
tasks.description: Remove the 'stagecoach:' prefix from the ErrEmptyMessage literal in internal/generate/finalize.go:45, since main.go:67 unconditionally prepends it. The sentinel identity (errors.Is) is preserved. Update any tests that assert on the literal string.

subtasks.title: Remove prefix from ErrEmptyMessage literal and update test assertions
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/minor_fixes.md §Issue 5. `var ErrEmptyMessage = errors.New("stagecoach: empty commit message — aborted")` at internal/generate/finalize.go:45. `cmd/stagecoach/main.go:67`: `fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "stagecoach: %v\n", err)` — prepends 'stagecoach: ' to any non-empty error. Result: 'stagecoach: stagecoach: empty commit message — aborted' (double prefix). The sentinel is propagated BARE via generate.go:486, decompose.go:409, message.go:254. exitcode.go:65 checks `errors.Is(err, generate.ErrEmptyMessage)`. Tests: internal/generate/generate_test.go:858-883 and internal/decompose/message_test.go reference ErrEmptyMessage.
2. INPUT: ErrEmptyMessage at finalize.go:45. main.go:67 error printing. All propagation paths return the BARE sentinel.
3. LOGIC: (a) Change finalize.go:45 from `errors.New("stagecoach: empty commit message — aborted")` to `errors.New("empty commit message — aborted")`. The sentinel identity is preserved (same *errors.errorString pointer; errors.Is still works). Both the --edit path (finalize.go:118) and the hooks-empty path (message.go:254 returns generate.ErrEmptyMessage) are fixed by this one change. (b) Search for any test that asserts on the literal string containing 'stagecoach: empty commit message' and update it to expect 'empty commit message' (without the prefix). Check internal/generate/generate_test.go and internal/decompose/message_test.go. (c) Run `go test ./internal/generate/ ./internal/decompose/ -v` to verify.
4. OUTPUT: The --edit abort now prints 'stagecoach: empty commit message — aborted' (single prefix). The sentinel identity is unchanged so errors.Is and exitcode.For still map it to exit 1.
5. DOCS: none — internal error message formatting, no user-facing config/API surface.

tasks.title: Fix auto-stage notice '(1 files)' grammar (Issue 6)
tasks.description: In internal/cmd/default_action.go:150, conditionally singularize 'files' to 'file' when the count is 1. Add a test for the n=1 singular case.

subtasks.title: Add conditional singular/plural in auto-stage notice and update tests
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/minor_fixes.md §Issue 6. The format string is at internal/cmd/default_action.go:150: `fmt.Fprintln(stderr, u.Yellow(fmt.Sprintf("Nothing staged — staging all changes (%d files).", n)))`. `n` comes from `g.StagedFileCount(ctx)` at line 143. The n==0 clean-tree path returns early at lines 147-150 (skips the notice). Existing test at internal/cmd/default_action_test.go:460 asserts 'Nothing staged — staging all changes (2 files).' (n=2 — stays green).
2. INPUT: The format string at default_action.go:150. The `u.Yellow` colorizer wrapper. The `n` int variable.
3. LOGIC: (a) At default_action.go:150, replace the hardcoded plural with a conditional: `noun := "files"; if n == 1 { noun = "file" }; fmt.Fprintln(stderr, u.Yellow(fmt.Sprintf("Nothing staged — staging all changes (%d %s).", n, noun)))`. (b) In default_action_test.go, add a test case for n=1 that asserts the notice contains '(1 file)' (singular). The existing n=2 test at line 460 continues to assert '(2 files)'. Run `go test ./internal/cmd/ -v -run TestRunDefault_AutoStageNotice`.
4. OUTPUT: The auto-stage notice now correctly prints '1 file' for a single file and 'N files' for multiple.
5. DOCS: none — minor UI text polish, no config/API surface.

milestones.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
milestones.description: Final documentation sweep: review and update cross-cutting docs (README.md, docs/how-it-works.md, docs/cli.md) that summarize the whole changeset. Per-file docs that were directly touched (docs/configuration.md token_limit floor, docs/providers.md commented-block note) are already handled as Mode A in their implementing subtasks.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: Review README.md, docs/how-it-works.md, and docs/cli.md for any references to the behaviors changed by this bugfix. Update where stale.

subtasks.title: Review and update README.md for changeset-level impacts
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/test_patterns.md §Documentation Files. README.md:66 mentions token_limit 'closed-loop guarantee'. README.md:242 mentions pi bare model error (FR-R5b). These may need updates to reflect the floor rejection (Issue 4) and the corrected bootstrap behavior (Issues 1 & 2). The changeset does NOT add new features — it fixes bugs so the implementation matches existing documentation. Most README references should already be accurate after the fixes (they describe correct behavior that was previously not implemented correctly).
2. INPUT: All implementing subtasks completed (P1.M1-P1.M3). The README.md file at the project root.
3. LOGIC: Review README.md sections that reference: (a) token_limit / closed-loop guarantee — if the floor rejection behavior (Issue 4) warrants a note, add it. (b) Config bootstrap / pi models — verify the description matches the corrected behavior. (c) config upgrade / backup — if README mentions config upgrade, verify it aligns with the backup fix. (d) Auto-stage notice — if README shows example output, verify grammar. If everything is already accurate, make no changes (but document that the review was done). Do NOT add feature blurbs for bugfixes — only correct stale references.
4. OUTPUT: README.md is accurate and consistent with the fixed implementation. If no changes needed, note that in the commit message.
5. DOCS: This IS the changeset-level documentation sync (Mode B).

subtasks.title: Review docs/how-it-works.md and docs/cli.md for changeset-level impacts
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/test_patterns.md §Documentation Files. docs/how-it-works.md may reference the diff capture pipeline and token_limit behavior. docs/cli.md documents CLI flags and may reference config init / config upgrade behavior. These docs may need updates for the token_limit floor rejection (Issue 4) or the config upgrade backup (Issue 3).
2. INPUT: All implementing subtasks completed. docs/how-it-works.md and docs/cli.md.
3. LOGIC: Review each file for references to: (a) token_limit / diff capture pipeline — add a note about the floor rejection if the topic is covered. (b) config init / config upgrade — verify descriptions match the corrected behavior (pi models blanked, backup created on upgrade). (c) Any example output that shows the auto-stage notice — verify grammar. If everything is already accurate, make no changes but document the review.
4. OUTPUT: docs/how-it-works.md and docs/cli.md are accurate. If no changes needed, note that.
5. DOCS: This IS the changeset-level documentation sync (Mode B).
